narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD weakness because DXY at 97.85 and falling, CSM shows USD at zero across both timeframes, and US-Iran peace deal hopes are actively suppressing safe-haven dollar demand
2. CHF and NZD strength because CHF leads H4 CSM at 100 while NZD dominates D1 CSM at 100, both independently pricing out dollar risk premium from opposite ends of the risk spectrum
3. JPY cross selling because USDJPY correlation cluster with CADJPY at 0.96 and EURJPY at 0.95 confirms broad yen weakness against all majors, consistent with VIX declining and equities holding gains

PRICE CONFIRMS

GBPUSD and EURUSD moving in lockstep at 0.94 correlation confirms this is a USD-driven selloff rather than idiosyncratic currency moves. USDCHF at strong sell minus five on H4 is the cleanest expression of dollar weakness, confirmed by its negative 0.76 correlation with EURUSD running in the expected direction. NZDUSD and AUDUSD are lagging their D1 CSM readings as those fade from highs, suggesting the commodity bloc rally may be losing internal momentum even as it tracks dollar weakness.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed but partially conflicted. VIX at 17.22 is declining and supportive, S&P 500 holds gains at 7371, but gold surging 1.76 percent and silver up 6.85 percent alongside a falling dollar introduces a safe-haven distortion that complicates a clean risk-on read.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold and risk-on: Gold at 4764 rising 1.76 percent contradicts pure risk-on positioning, suggesting dollar distrust rather than simple risk appetite is the primary engine
2. NZD CSM divergence: NZD leads D1 CSM at 100 but is fading on H4 at 76, meaning the pair is overbought relative to its timeframe trend and prone to reversal without fresh catalyst

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: USDCHF down because all four sources align on USD zero and CHF at H4 maximum strength - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: NZDUSD up because USD weakness dominant and NZD D1 CSM still strongest currency reading - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: AUDUSD because AUD CSM fading on both timeframes while oil dropping 3.71 percent undermines commodity support
Watch: DXY breaking below 97.50 confirms primary USDCHF trade and validates the USD selloff extending into the London session open

themes.theme: US-Iran peace deal hopes driving risk-on sentiment
Fed higher-for-longer stance supporting USD strength structurally
UK election risks and fiscal concerns weighing on Sterling
Central bank divergence: Riksbank on hold, Norges Bank hiking, NBP on hold
Oil supply shocks and commodity weakness pressuring commodity currencies
Weak US economic data: jobless claims, productivity miss, strong construction
Mexican peso weakness continuing amid structural concerns
European data mixed with fiscal and construction risks emerging

key_observation: Strong risk-on sentiment driven by US-Iran peace deal hopes is overriding structural USD support from Fed higher-for-longer policy. Dollar weakness is broad-based with commodity and risk currencies rallying. However, Fed fundamentals remain supportive for USD on any pullback in geopolitical optimism.
watch: Confirmation of US-Iran deal terms and durability of risk appetite; Fed speakers addressing higher-for-longer commitment; UK election outcome and fiscal trajectory; commodity price stabilization especially oil; eurozone construction and fiscal data evolution
